summary:
Based on Agent1’s analysis, acellular dermis is not considered part of active tissue engineering because it lacks living cells essential for generating new tissues; instead, other methods involve actively participating biological elements crucial for regenerative processes. To be precise:

Acellular dermal matrices do not qualify as active tissue engineering due to the absence of vital living cells needed for tissue creation, distinguishing them from techniques involving more biologically dynamic approaches.
